member.name: Noah Law
member.role: MP for St Austell and Newquay
member.synopsis: Noah Law is the Labour MP for St Austell and Newquay , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Breakfast Clubs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will take steps to ensure Free Breakfast Clubs use food which is procured from British sources.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Food served at free breakfast clubs must meet the School Food Standards. The standards set out the types of food that should be served and how often and are designed to help children develop healthy eating habits and ensure they receive the nutrition need...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Breakfast Clubs: Nutrition
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure Free Breakfast Clubs include the most nutritional food possible.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Food served at free breakfast clubs must meet the School Food Standards. The standards set out the types of food that should be served and how often and are designed to help children develop healthy eating habits and ensure they receive the nutrition need...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Clean Energy: Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of levels of private sector investment in clean energy industries since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since July 2024, over £100 billion of private investment has been announced into UK clean energy industries, with more money coming into this country by way of foreign investment in clean energy than any other industrial strategy sector over the past 10-y...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Jet Skis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, if they have considered applying an age limit for jet ski use.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has no current plans to introduce an age limit for jet ski use. Powers are available to ports and local authorities to place restrictions on use, and they remain best placed to respond appropriately to local conditions. Nation...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Medical Examinations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether his Department plans to review the use of video surveillance and media appearances in disability benefit decisions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not use video surveillance or media appearances for routine disability benefit decisions.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Property Management Companies: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the potential economic and market benefit from the establishment of a regulator for the Property Management companies sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 85213 on 4 November 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Coronavirus: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential merits of widening the eligibility of COVID vaccines to people with Multiple Sclerosis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to protecting those most vulnerable to COVID-19 through vaccination, as guided by the independent Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI). The primary aim of the national COVID-19 vaccination programme remains th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Family Courts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what support the Government is giving to families in the Family Court who have suffered abuse by non-resident parents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that victims of domestic abuse, including children, are properly supported in the Family Courts. This is regardless of whether the abusive parent is resident or non-resident at the time of the case.Family Courts hav...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Wales, what steps she is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help improve the Welsh rail network.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I was pleased to join the Prime Minister in Taffs Well earlier this month to announce seven new stations in Wales and a generational funding commitment to the Welsh rail network and a pipeline of projects valued at £14 billion.The Prime Minister endorsed ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Wales Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what guidance or requirements exist for NHS trusts, GP practices and other health providers to ensure that medical records, appointment letters and other clinical correspondence about a child are s
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England has a range of guidance online relating to communicating with patients, and the sharing of medical records for direct care. NHS England continues to support the secure sharing of patient information to improve safety, reduce the duplication of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of widening the eligibility of the NHS Low Income Scheme to include those who are on a state pension.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such assessment has been made. People aged 60 years old and over are automatically entitled to free National Health Service prescriptions and free NHS eye tests.People on the state pension can apply for the NHS Low Income Scheme and depending on their ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: New Businesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps his Department has taken with Homes England to help support start-up builders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Small and medium sized housebuilders are essential to meeting the government’s housing ambitions and supporting local economies.The government is acting to support SME housebuilders by increasing their access to land, providing further financial assistanc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Planning: Nature Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to ensure the widest set of projects as part of biodiversity net-gain calculations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Biodiversity net gain (BNG) applies to every planning permission in England granted under the Town and Country Planning Act 1990, unless exempted. The Government has announced plans to remove BNG requirements for sites under 0.2 hectares, and that BNG wil...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Regional Planning and Development: Cornwall
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to provide capacity funding to local authorities for strategic economic development in Cornwall.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is establishing a £30 million fund to invest in Cornwall’s comparative sectoral advantages, including critical minerals, renewable energy and marine innovation, subject to a full business case. Funding will be devolved to Cornwall Council, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Kernow Industrial Growth Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to ensure that the Kernow Industrial Growth Fund can be deployed as investment capital which can be recouped by Cornwall County Council for future project
writtenQuestions.answerText: Funding will be devolved to Cornwall Council, in line with the Government’s devolution agenda, empowering local leaders to invest in projects that best meet local needs. Final release of funding will be subject to a robust business case.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Royal Mail
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to help ensure Royal Mail meets its statutory obligations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofcom, as the independent regulator of postal services, has powers to investigate and take enforcement action should Royal Mail fail to achieve its obligations without good justification. Ofcom fined Royal Mail £21 million for failing to meet its quality ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce burdens on NHS dentistry services, especially by ensuring dentists who are qualified to practice in other countries can be fast-tracked for qualification to practi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The General Dental Council (GDC) regulates United Kingdom dentistry and sets the standards for all applicants to its registers. Routes to registration for overseas qualified dentists are set out in legislation.The Government is working with the GDC to inc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Developing Countries: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what progress has been made towards global debt reform in discussions with the London Coalition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to working with our international partners and the private sector to tackle unsustainable debt. The London Coalition for Sustainable Sovereign Debt, initiated last year, continues to make progress on bolstering the private sect...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Public Sector
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she plans to ensure that food purchased by the public sector recognises seasonality of produce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government Buying Standards for Food and Catering Services (GBSF) already encourage public sector caterers to include seasonally available ingredients in their menus. The Government is currently considering options, including updating the GBSF to furt...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Older People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his Department plans to reform planning rules and permitted development rights to facilitate a wider range of housing for elderly people, including modern, low-maintenanc
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is currently consulting on changes to the National Planning Policy Framework (NPPF), including new policies designed to support the delivery of housing that meets the needs of different groups such as older people. The consultation can be f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
